title: [lora_l2.c:1047-1050] lichen_lora_l2_is_running and needs_reinit have no memory barrier
description:
Gap: The public state query functions read atomic_t without memory barriers.

What: Lines 1047-1055 define is_running() and needs_reinit() which read current_state via atomic_get(). On multi-core systems (which LICHEN rejects via BUILD_ASSERT), this could return stale values.

Why it matters: While the BUILD_ASSERT at lichen_l2.c:375-376 prevents multi-core builds, the is_running() function is part of the public API and could be called from interrupt context or by code that doesn't know about the single-core constraint. The atomic_get() provides no ordering guarantees beyond atomicity. For robustness, the API docs should clarify these functions are only valid when called from thread context on single-core.
close_reason: Acceptable: BUILD_ASSERT at lichen_l2.c:374-376 rejects multi-core builds at compile time. On single-core (the only supported config), atomic_get provides sufficient ordering. The public API implicitly inherits this constraint. Adding docs would be belt-and-suspenders; the compile-time enforcement is the real protection.
